description: Lightweight qualitative output suite for the issue-shaping skill. These cases are meant for human review after running the skill on representative issue refinement, decomposition recommendation, and readiness-verdict tasks.

assertion_definitions.scope_bounded: The output sharpens the issue into one coherent work boundary rather than repeating a broad initiative.
assertion_definitions.non_goals_explicit: The output clearly states what the shaped issue does not include.
assertion_definitions.acceptance_criteria_usable: The acceptance criteria are observable and useful for downstream execution or verification.
assertion_definitions.dependencies_visible: Dependencies, blockers, or coupled work are made visible instead of hidden.
assertion_definitions.readiness_verdict_clear: The output ends with an explicit readiness verdict.
assertion_definitions.routing_honest: When the issue is not ready, the output routes to clarify, exploration, or decomposition instead of pretending otherwise.

cases.name: Turn a normalized issue into a clear execution slice
cases.prompt: Take this normalized issue and make it execution-ready without drifting into a full plan.
success_criteria: Narrows the issue to one coherent job.
Adds explicit non-goals and stronger acceptance criteria.
Ends with a useful next workflow.

cases.name: Broad issue should get a split recommendation
cases.repo_archetype: Monorepo or platform repo with cross-cutting backlog items
cases.prompt: Shape this broad platform issue and say clearly whether it should split.
success_criteria: Does not fake readiness for an obviously broad issue.
Explains why the issue should split by boundary, owner, or success criteria.
Leaves a clearer parent-child direction than the original issue.

cases.name: Weak evidence should route to exploration honestly
cases.repo_archetype: Repo where issue drafts often arrive with partial evidence
cases.prompt: Shape this issue even though the current evidence is thin and we are not sure which subsystem owns the problem.
success_criteria: States what evidence is missing and why it blocks honest shaping.
Recommends a smallest next exploration step.
Keeps the issue from drifting into made-up certainty.
